channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lateef Adedimeji Explains Why He Cries In Movies To Stand Out From Odunlade Adekola
item.description: Nollywood actor Lateef Adedimeji has shared how his signature crying scenes in movies helped him stand out from his colleague Odunlade Adekola. During an interview with Morayo Afolabi Brown on her You...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler (1951–2026): Welsh singer and rock icon who defined a generation
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the iconic Welsh singer known for her raspy voice and powerful performances, has passed away at the age of 75 following a short illness. Her career spanned decades, marked by hits like '...

item.title: Rare dolphin sightings in B.C. linked to El Niño and climate change
item.description: A rare sighting of long-beaked common dolphins has been reported in British Columbia's waters, marking an extraordinary event due to their rarity in temperate regions. Jackie Hildering of the Marine E...

item.title: France advances to World Cup semifinals after dominant win over Morocco
item.description: France secured a 2-0 victory against Morocco in their World Cup quarterfinal clash, advancing to the semifinals with confidence. Coach Didier Deschamps emphasized the team's resilience despite missed ...

item.title: Maharashtra forms seven-member panel to draft Uniform Civil Code
item.description: Maharashtra has formed a seven-member committee headed by retired Supreme Court judge Ranjana Prakash Desai to prepare a draft for implementing a Uniform Civil Code in the state. Chief Minister Devend...

item.title: Barbie Almalbis Clarifies Barbie’s Cradle Never Broke Up, Emphasizes Music as Bond
item.description: Barbie Almalbis, a prominent Filipino musician, recently addressed rumors about the dissolution of her band Barbie’s Cradle. In an interview, she clarified that the group never officially broke up, em...

item.title: South Africa's Orange Basin Energy Deal Faces Geopolitical Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the tension between South Africa's commercial energy decisions and its geopolitical stance, particularly regarding an Israeli firm's involvement in the Orange Basin oil explorati...

item.title: Fidji Simo Steps Down from OpenAI’s Leadership Role Amid Health Challenges
item.description: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's second-in-command, is transitioning to a part-time advisory role after her prolonged medical leave due to a neuroimmune condition. Her departure marks a significant shift for the ...

item.title: The Controversial 1994 Wedding of Anna Nicole Smith and J. Howard Marshall II
item.description: On June 27, 1994, 26-year-old model and actress Anna Nicole Smith married 89-year-old billionaire oil tycoon J. Howard Marshall II at the White Dove Wedding Chapel in Houston, Texas. The ceremony beca...

item.title: Fact Check: COA's Unmodified Opinion on OVP Doesn't Automatically Clear VP Sara Duterte
item.description: The article clarifies that an unmodified audit opinion from the Commission on Audit (COA) does not automatically mean Vice President Sara Duterte has been cleared of wrongdoing. While the OVP received...

item.title: Stan Nze Advocates for Leader Focused on National Progress Over Tribal Ties
item.description: Nollywood actor Stan Nze has expressed his preference for Nigerian leaders who prioritize national progress over tribal or regional affiliations. In a social media post, he emphasized that his support...

item.title: Fact-checker criticizes JD Vance for claiming credit in speech about a federal fraud case
item.description: The article reports on criticism directed at Vice President JD Vance following a speech in Milwaukee in which he discussed a government-funded prenatal healthcare program for at-risk mothers and refer...

item.title: Oil Market Adjusts to Oversupply Amid Geopolitical Volatility
item.description: The article highlights the disconnect between geopolitical events, such as tanker attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, and the underlying trends in the oil market. Despite spikes in prices due to attacks ...

item.title: Fact Check: No Evidence Links COVID-19 Vaccines to Premature Death
item.description: The article debunks a false claim circulating online that COVID-19 vaccines cause recipients to die by 2030. It explains that there is no scientific evidence supporting this assertion. Multiple studie...

item.title: Migrants Contest ICE Shooting Account in Houston Incident
item.description: Three men arrested during an immigration operation in Houston that resulted in the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo are disputing the Department of Homeland Security's account of the incident....

item.title: Zanzibar's Ruling Party and Opposition Agree to Reconciliation and Institutional Reforms
item.description: The ruling Chama cha Mapinduzi (CCM) and the main opposition party ACT Wazalendo have signed a joint statement aimed at resolving the political deadlock following the disputed 2025 elections. The agre...

item.title: Bungie Director's Lawsuit With Sony Resolved, Credits Added To Marathon
item.description: Christopher Barrett, a veteran of Bungie, has settled his legal dispute with Sony after being terminated in 2024 amid allegations of misconduct. Barrett claimed Sony's termination was a breach of cont...

item.title: Telstra's network outage highlights leadership challenges during executive absence
item.description: The article discusses how Telstra's technical issues, exacerbated by the absence of CEO Vicki Brady during a critical period, have sparked broader conversations about corporate governance. While polit...

item.title: Baba Talisha updates on daughter's ADHD and dyslexia diagnosis with next steps
item.description: Baba Talisha has shared an update about his daughter, Baby Talisha, revealing she was diagnosed with ADHD and dyslexia following a comprehensive assessment at the Kenya Institute of Special Education ...

item.title: Spar Issues Recall for Multiple Yoghurt Products Due to Spoilage Concerns
item.description: The National Consumer Commission (NCC) has initiated a recall of several Spar yoghurt products nationwide due to a production machine malfunction. The affected items include fat-free, low-fat, double ...
